AMD.reasoning: AMD has closed above the $352.99 resistance level (10x tested, volume-confirmed hourly pivot high) on both Apr 30 ($354.49) and May 1 ($360.54), technically constituting a daily close breakout. However, the breakout FAILS the mandatory volume confirmation requirement: daily relative volume on May 1 is only 0.86x the 20-day average (0.86x vs the required 1.5x minimum). This is actually weaker than the prior run (1.05x), making this a deteriorating-volume breakout — the exact pattern most prone to failure. Range boundaries: consolidation was $328.81 (hourly support, 6x tested, volume-confirmed) to $352.99 (hourly resistance), giving a range height of ~$24.18. Measured-move target if volume were confirmed: $352.99 + $24.18 = $377.17. BB width = 0.6453 — already wide and expanding, confirming the squeeze has already fully fired; there is no coil left to release. ADX = 76.53 signals an extreme established trend, not a fresh breakout setup — this is not my setup type. RSI-14 = 83.49 and RSI-21 = 86.42 indicate deep statistical overextension; Z-score = 2.5 is above the +2.0 mean-reversion warning zone. Hourly volume = 1.48x (borderline, just under threshold) with OBV diverging bearishly from price — no institutional accumulation signal. Earnings binary May 5 (after close) creates undefined gap risk in both directions and is a hard veto on new swing entries per standing policy. Lessons cross-check: the recent NVDA EMA pullback failure at comparable RSI (81-87) warns against chasing overbought setups even with valid trend structure. No prior call to grade (prediction_grading: null). Standing aside: re-entry paths are (1) post-earnings gap-and-hold above $352.99 with 1.5x+ daily volume, or (2) organic pullback to $308-$315 EMA zone with bullish hourly MACD recross.

AMD.reasoning: VOTE COUNT: 5 of 5 agents neutral — unanimous stand-aside driven by a single overriding factor: AMD reports Q1 2026 earnings tomorrow May 5 after market close. This is not a close call. Every agent — trend, mean-reversion, breakout, catalyst, and macro — independently arrived at the same conclusion: the binary event veto overrides all technical signals in both directions. No new swing entry is warranted. EXISTING POSITION CONTEXT (critical): The portfolio already holds an AMD short entered at $354.49 on 2026-05-01 (1 share, stop $372.31, target $277.74, 4.31:1 R/R). Current price $360.54 puts this short underwater by -$6.05 (-1.71%). The short is now inside its valid stop range and within $11.77 of the $372.31 stop. With earnings AMC tomorrow, the position faces gap risk of 15-20% in either direction — a bullish beat could push AMD to $400+ and blow through the stop; a miss could gap to $310 and deliver a large gain. This is exactly why the position was sized at 1 share (minimum exposure to the binary). ACTION: Do NOT add to the short. Do NOT open a long. Hold the existing 1-share short as structured. TECHNICAL CONTEXT (informational only — no actionable entry): AMD closed at $360.54. EMA structure is textbook bullish (10 EMA 324.49 > 21 EMA 292.67 > 50 EMA 255.96). ADX 76.53 is the highest ever recorded for AMD in this system. RSI-14 83.49, RSI-21 86.42 — extreme overbought. Z-score vs 50-period 2.5. Daily Bollinger pct_b 0.91. Hourly momentum is cooling: ROC 5d -2.56%, MACD histogram -0.018, OBV diverging bearishly — the only early warning that the parabolic move may be stalling. Breakout above $352.99 (10x-tested resistance) was on 0.86x relative volume — disqualified. MACRO OVERLAY: Mixed-to-cautious. Iran missile escalation May 4 (Jask island strike near Strait of Hormuz) reversed the prior risk-on baseline. Fed on hold at 3.5-3.75% with Powell transition uncertainty (out May 15, Warsh likely successor). This macro environment does not support initiating new swing positions on a +71% 21-day mover. POST-EARNINGS FRAMEWORK (both agents and wiki agree): Bull re-entry: gap-and-hold above $352.99 on 1.5x+ daily volume with hourly MACD recross → entry zone $316-$335 (10-EMA / Fib 23.6% confluence), target $390-$409, stop $308. Bear fade continuation: sustained rejection below $352.99 with a confirmed daily reversal candle → adds to existing short thesis, potential to add 1 share to the short at $350-$355, stop $372.31 (unchanged), target $277.74. LESSONS FLAG: Only closed lesson in the system is NVDA EMA pullback (RSI 81-87, overbought, AI capex thesis) — result was a stop-out at -$63.20. AMD's RSI 83.49 is directly analogous. Confidence dialed down to 22 to reflect: (1) the binary event dominance, (2) the existing underwater short creating directional exposure already, (3) zero historical win rate on the only analogous setup in the lessons file.
AMD.key_conflicts: No directional conflict exists — all 5 agents independently concluded neutral/stand-aside. The most instructive near-miss conflict is between swing_trend_momentum (bullish on trend structure, ADX 76.53, textbook EMA alignment, expanding daily momentum) and swing_mean_reversion (bearish on statistical exhaustion, Z-score 2.5, RSI 86, 53% above 50-SMA, prior bearish fade attempt stopped out on May 1). In any other week, this would be the central tension: a trend so strong it defies mean-reversion, vs. an extension so extreme reversion is statistically inevitable. But the earnings binary on May 5 AMC makes this conflict moot — it is simultaneously the wrong time to chase the trend AND the wrong time to fade it, because a 15-20% gap in either direction can invalidate both setups instantly. The secondary conflict worth noting: swing_catalyst_news is net-positive on news flow (Intel Q1 beat as AMD read-through, three analyst Buy reiterations at $375, Meta $60B infrastructure deal still active) but explicitly downgrades its own bullish thesis because the positive news is fully priced in at $360 — stock is trading above every major analyst target ($293 avg consensus, $358 Evercore ISI bull case). Macro_context provides the veto confirmation: asymmetry math produces only 1.55:1 R/R (below the 2:1 minimum), and a miss-and-lower scenario on Q2 guidance (25% tariff headwinds on AI chip exports) could push AMD to $310, flipping asymmetry negative. Portfolio context adds a further constraint: existing AMD short at $354.49 is already deployed, book is 3 long / 3 short (balanced but full), and adding any new AMD exposure before the earnings print stacks binary risk on binary risk.

AMD.reasoning: TREND ASSESSMENT: Unambiguously bullish — EMA alignment is textbook perfect (10 EMA 324.49 > 21 EMA 292.67 > 50 EMA 255.96), and ADX 76.53 is at an extreme rarely seen for AMD (wiki confirms this is the highest ever recorded in this system). Price has made a strong series of higher highs and higher lows over the past 5 sessions, closing at $360.54 vs a low of $310 less than 3 weeks ago. MOMENTUM ASSESSMENT: Daily momentum is strongly expanding — ROC 5d +3.66%, ROC 10d +29.51%, ROC 21d +71.51% (all positive and sequentially expanding, the ideal pattern). Daily MACD histogram +6.89 with line above signal confirms acceleration. However, hourly momentum is beginning to diverge: hourly ROC 5d -2.56%, 10d -1.3% (negative on short-term), hourly MACD histogram slightly negative (-0.018), and hourly OBV is diverging from price — early warning signs of intraday exhaustion. NO VALID ENTRY: Strategy requires a pullback to the 10 or 21 EMA to enter. Price at $360.54 is 11.1% above the 10 EMA and 23.2% above the 21 EMA — no pullback has occurred. Chasing here violates strategy rules. OVEREXTENSION: RSI 14 at 83.49, RSI 21 at 86.42 (extreme overbought); Z-score 50 at 2.5; Bollinger %B at 0.91. EARNINGS VETO: AMD reports earnings on May 5 (tomorrow), making any new swing position a binary earnings bet, not a trend-momentum trade. Wiki explicitly flagged this: 'earnings binary May 5 vetoes all swing positions in either direction.' LESSONS: Prior EMA-pullback trade on NVDA under similar RSI 81-87 conditions (overbought, no confirmation candle) resulted in a loss. Pattern table shows 0% win rate on the only recent comparable setup. MACRO: Iran escalation on May 4 returned macro to mixed-cautious from prior risk-on. Conclusion: trend and momentum are both excellent but there is no actionable entry — the stock is far from any EMA pullback zone, earnings create a binary event veto, and hourly divergences warn of near-term fatigue. Wait for post-earnings resolution: a gap-and-hold above $352.99 with 1.5x+ volume, or a pullback into the $308-$330 EMA zone for a re-entry.

MACRO REGIME: Mixed-to-cautious. The prior risk-on baseline (Iran peace talks, S&P 500 ATH 7,230 on May 1) has reversed as of May 4: reports of missile strikes on a US warship near Jask island have reignited Strait of Hormuz closure risk, pushed oil higher, and pulled Dow futures lower. Fed is on hold at 3.5-3.75% with a leadership transition (Powell out May 15, Warsh as likely successor) introducing additional policy uncertainty. This is a transitional-to-risk-off regime, not the clean risk-on backdrop that justifies chasing a +71% 21-day move. RELATIVE STRENGTH: AMD has massively outperformed — ROC 21d=+71.51%, ROC 10d=+29.51%. RSI-14=83.49, RSI-21=86.42, ADX=76.53. Price is 53% above the 50-SMA (z-score 2.5) and at Bollinger pct_b=0.91 — extreme stretch by any measure. While outperformance signals sector inflows, this is LATE in a sector rotation cycle, not early, which is the trigger condition for my macro veto. The wiki thesis explicitly flags: 'Four consecutive runs produced the highest momentum readings in the watchlist. The stock has now run +74% in 21 trading days to $354 ATH.' Entering at all-time highs on day 22 of a 74% run is chasing, not leading. Hourly momentum is already decelerating: ROC 5d=-2.56%, ROC 10d=-1.3%. Hourly OBV is diverging from price (bearish divergence). Hourly MACD histogram at -0.02 (flat/turning). ASYMMETRY MATH: Upside to Fib ext 127.2% = $409 (~13.7% from $360). Nearest hourly support = $328.81 (~8.8% downside). Conservative stop near $328 = ~8.8% risk. 13.7% upside vs 8.8% downside = 1.55:1 asymmetry — below the 2:1 minimum bar. If price flushes to $310 on an earnings miss (the fat-tail risk with AMD reporting May 5 AMC), downside expands to ~14%, flipping asymmetry negative. BINARY EVENT OVERRIDE: AMD reports Q1 earnings tomorrow May 5 after market close. This is a regime-dominating binary event that makes the asymmetry calculation unreliable — the stock can gap 15-20% in either direction overnight, instantly invalidating any stop. The wiki flags this explicitly: 'Earnings on May 5 create a binary event that dominates near-term risk.' Entering a swing position the day before earnings at all-time highs with RSI 83 violates capital preservation discipline. LESSONS FLAG: The most recent lessons entry (NVDA, 2026-04-30) is a direct analogue: RSI 81-87, bullish AI capex thesis, macro stress — result was a stop-out. I am not repeating that setup without adjustment. PASS CRITERIA: The setup fails on (1) asymmetry below 2:1 threshold, (2) binary earnings event tomorrow making stops unreliable, (3) late-cycle relative-strength entry (day 22 of +74% move), (4) macro regime is cautious/transitional not clean risk-on. POST-EARNINGS OPPORTUNITY: If AMD beats and dips to $316-$328 (wiki-flagged entry zone: 10-EMA / Fib 23.6% confluence), the asymmetry and timing improve materially — that would be the correct entry window with ~20% upside to $409 vs ~5% downside to $310.
AMD.timeframe: 5-10 trading days (post-earnings, contingent on May 5 AMC reaction)

AMD.reasoning: Branch A (fade the extreme) shows multiple statistical overextension signals: daily Z-score 2.50 (above the >2 threshold), RSI-14 83.49 and RSI-21 86.42 (well above the 75 overbought threshold), and distance from 50-SMA at 53.18% (extreme, far exceeding the >10% notable threshold). However, three factors collectively veto a bearish fade. First, ADX 76.53 is the highest ever recorded for AMD in this system — a trend this strong can stay extended far longer than mean-reversion timing allows, and the prior bearish fade at $354 was stopped out on the same day (2026-05-01, stopped at $360, mae 2.48%). The adjustment from the stopped-out call: I am not repeating the bearish fade when ADX is at an all-time system extreme with no reversal candle confirmation. Second, there is no reversal candle in the 5-bar recent price series — five consecutive closes with rising highs. The prompt explicitly says 'WAIT for a reversal candle or momentum shift before entering — do not catch a falling knife' (or in the bullish-fade case, a parabolic top). Third — and most critical — AMD earnings are tomorrow May 5, confirmed in wiki context across multiple runs. The wiki's two-way post-earnings framework explicitly vetoes all swing positions in either direction ahead of the binary. Branch B (dip-buy at Fib) does not fire: price is AT the recent swing high ($362.79), not in a pullback. Hourly indicators show partial cooling (RSI-21 68.0 vs daily 83+, Z-score 1.33 vs daily 2.50, MACD histogram slightly negative at -0.0179, OBV diverging from price on hourly) but this intraday softness does not constitute a clean Fib-confluence setup. Wiki two-way post-earnings re-entry framework: bull leg re-entry $316-$335 (10-EMA zone) on gap-and-hold with 1.5x+ volume; bear fade $355-$368 on confirmed daily reversal candle with 2:1+ R/R. Wait for post-earnings resolution before taking any directional swing position.

AMD.reasoning: CATALYST WINDOW: Q1 2026 earnings in 1 trading day (May 5, 2026, after market close, 5 PM ET) — this is the live binary catalyst. High-weight event, but timing creates a classic 'buy the rumor, sell the news' risk. NET NEWS SENTIMENT: Positive on balance — Intel Q1 2026 blowout (+24.89%) validates x86 AI CPU demand as a read-through for AMD; DA Davidson (Buy/$375), Susquehanna (Buy/$375), and Envision Research all upgraded this week. Meta $60B infrastructure deal still active as structural bull. One downgrade: Northland to Market Perform/$260. However, AMD is now trading ABOVE the average analyst consensus PT (~$293) and above Evercore ISI's bull target ($358) at ~$360. The positive news is well-priced-in. INSIDER ACTIVITY: Quiet in the last 30 days (no recent insider trades in facts bundle). Wiki notes CEO Lisa Su sold ~$16M in March 2026; board-level insider distribution 2:1 sellers vs buyers. Net: slightly bearish lean, not a near-term directional signal but reinforces 'don't chase at ATH' posture. TECHNICAL SETUP — POOR/EXTENDED: Daily RSI 14 = 83.49 (severely overbought), RSI 7 = 76.85, RSI 21 = 86.42. Price $360.54 sits 53.18% above the 50-SMA and 22.23% above the 20-EMA. Z-score vs 50-period = 2.5 (extreme stretch). Daily Bollinger pct_b = 0.9115 — near the upper band ($376.81). The ADX at 76.53 confirms a powerful trend but also signals potential exhaustion at extremes. Hourly MACD histogram is slightly negative (-0.0179) suggesting near-term momentum fading. Hourly OBV diverging from price (bearish divergence signal). AMD hit a recent high of $362.79 — just below the hourly pivot resistance at $352.99, with price briefly exceeding it intraday. RISK/REWARD ASYMMETRY: Pre-catalyst run of ~76% in April means enormous optimism is priced in. Revenue consensus ~$9.84B; beat required. Primary risk: beat-and-lower on Q2 guidance due to 25% tariff headwinds on AI chip exports. Fib extension target to 1.272x is $409.01 — but from a base that already stretched 76%. LESSONS APPLIED: Per wiki lessons, NVDA catalyst trade (April 30) stopped out at RSI 81-87 range — identical overbought condition to AMD's current RSI 83.49. That loss pattern directly matches today's AMD setup. Confidence dialed down accordingly. CONCLUSION: Catalyst exists and is imminent (1 trading day), news flow is net positive, but (1) chart is severely extended — classic 'already exhausted going into the catalyst' disqualifier per strategy rules; (2) stock trades above all analyst consensus PTs, implying sell-the-news risk on even a beat; (3) insider distribution leans negative; (4) recent NVDA lesson shows this exact RSI regime leads to stops being triggered. Per strategy rules: 'A catalyst alone is not enough if the chart is already extended.' Stand aside. No identifiable low-risk entry with favorable R/R ahead of binary catalyst at these levels.
